sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27
sources.population: Adults with suspected or established hypertension.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth hypertension assessment and follow-up; partial for a general setting-agnostic follow-up workflow.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth hypertension scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Recommendation — blood-pressure assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Management — monitoring and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes monitoring phases, clinician-judged laboratory review, lifestyle domains, and monthly review context.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Referral Criteria and Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — tests required for hypertension
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.
